description: Renames the display file_name column on the EDMSFileProcessing SQL row from the File Queue UI Edit Metadata modal. This is a display-name-only change — it does not move the Azure blob or the SharePoint source file. The rename fires in parallel with the Azure Search metadata update (non-fatal: .catch() prevents it blocking the main update). The PATCH route delegates all DB work to FileQueueService.rename_file() which mutates the ORM object and commits, returning the previous name for audit/UI feedback.
db_operations: SELECT FROM doc_intel_files_processing WHERE id = file_record_id
UPDATE doc_intel_files_processing SET file_name = new_file_name WHERE id = file_record_id

error_handling.frontend_non_fatal: renameFile is wrapped in .catch() — failure is swallowed, does not block Azure Search update
error_handling.route_404: FileNotFoundError from service is caught and mapped to HTTPException(404)
error_handling.route_422: Pydantic min_length=1 rejects blank file_name before route runs
error_handling.route_500: Unexpected exceptions caught and raised as HTTPException(500)

key_design_decisions: Display-name only — no blob or SharePoint rename
Non-fatal on frontend — .catch() prevents rename failure from blocking Azure update
FileNotFoundError not HTTPException in service — HTTP is route's concern
previous_name returned for UI feedback / potential undo
mode=before validator — whitespace stripped before min_length check
No explicit rollback — SQLAlchemy session lifecycle handles uncommitted changes on exception
